mission.title: tikv: TiKV running over 2 years may panic
mission.description: TiKV running over 2 years may panic. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify tikv troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your tikv installation:
```bash
tikv-server --version
```
Look for error: `:AssertUnwindSafe<F> as core::ops::function::FnOnce<()>>::call_once\n             at /rustc/2faabf579323f5252329264cc53b`

steps.title: Review tikv configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant tikv configuration:
## Bug Report



### What version of TiKV are you using?
`nightly`(this case also appears on v3.0.5)

### What operating system and CPU are you using?


### Steps to reproduce


Running TiKV for 795 days, then some RPC failure may cause

steps.title: Apply the fix for TiKV running over 2 years may panic
steps.description:
### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: Close #11940

What's Changed:

```commit-message
*: hotfix panic from tokio-timer by enlarging the length of the level vector
```

`tokio-timer 0.2.13`'s time wheel works when the elapsed time since the process starting is less than `2^36
```yaml
tiup playground --tiflash=0 --monitor=false
sudo kill {pid_of_pd}
sudo ./watchmaker -pid {pid_of_tikv} -sec_delta 68719436 -clk_ids=CLOCK_MONOTONIC
```

steps.title: Upgrade tikv to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade tikv:
```bash
# Check current version
tikv-server --version
# Follow the project's upgrade guide at https://github.com/tikv/tikv
```

steps.title: Confirm TiKV running over 2 years may panic is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test tikv to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that `:AssertUnwindSafe<F> as core::ops::function::FnOnce<()>>::call_once\n             at /rustc/2faabf579323f5252329264cc53b` no longer appears.

resolution.summary:
### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: Close #11940

What's Changed:

```commit-message
*: hotfix panic from tokio-timer by enlarging the length of the level vector
```

`tokio-timer 0.2.13`'s time wheel works when the elapsed time since the process starting is less than `2^36 ms`(about 795 days), otherwise, it may panic.
